title: Race-Lap Query Ranker As A Blend-Only Specialist
summary: Pit decisions are relative within a race state: among cars on the same race/lap, who is most likely to stop next.
rationale: Pit decisions are relative within a race state: among cars on the same race/lap, who is most likely to stop next. Learning-to-rank frameworks explicitly optimize within query groups, which is a better fit for this local ordering problem than pure global classification.
implementation_hint: Create query groups like Year x Race x LapNumber, or Year x Race x LapNumber x dry/wet regime. Train an XGBRanker or CatBoost ranking model with the existing numeric features plus peer-relative ranks/percentiles inside each query, then blend its scores with the base classifier instead of replacing the classifier.
expected_effect: Better within-lap ordering, especially in crowded strategy windows where multiple cars have similar absolute features but different relative urgency.
risk: Ranking loss is not the Kaggle metric, so this is usually a specialist model not a standalone winner; folds must keep entire query groups intact or the signal will leak.
